title: Tapinanthus lecardii
taxonomy: Tapinanthus lecardii is classified as a species of parasitic plant in the family Loranthaceae. This family consists of approximately 73 genera and over 900 species. The Loranthaceae family is part of the order Santalales, which includes other parasitic and hemiparasitic plants. The Santalales order is a part of the larger clade Santalales, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their parasitic or hemiparasitic nature. Tapinanthus lecardii is native to tropical regions of West Africa.
